title: Error Taxonomy, Wrapping, Retry Decisions, User Recovery, and Exit · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers error taxonomy, wrapping, retry decisions, user recovery, and exit into a changed causal recoverable Pokedex error contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of causal recoverable Pokedex error contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Classify input, not found, protocol, timeout, cancellation, transport, decode, invariant, persistence, and internal errors by caller decision.
Wrap context with percent-w while preserving sentinel or typed matching and avoid exposing unstable internal prose as API.
Retry only explicitly transient idempotent work under attempt, time, spacing, cancellation, and fair-use budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Error Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 2585 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify causal failures, preserve matching, bound retry, and isolate session recovery; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify input, not found, protocol, timeout, cancellation, transport, decode, invariant, persistence, and internal errors by caller decision.
